answer:
We have that \begin{align*}
\sin^2 x + \sin^2 3x + \sin^2 5x + \sin^2 7x &= 2\\
\Rightarrow\qquad \frac{1 - \cos 2x}{2} + \frac{1 - \cos 6x}{2} + \frac{1 - \cos 10x}{2} + \frac{1 - \cos 14x}{2} &= 2\\
\Rightarrow\qquad 4 - \cos 2x - \cos 6x - \cos 10x - \cos 14x &= 4\\
\Rightarrow\qquad - \cos 2x - \cos 6x - \cos 10x - \cos 14x &= 0\\
\Rightarrow\qquad - \cos 2x - \cos 14x - \cos 6x - \cos 10x &= 0\\
\Rightarrow\qquad - 2\cos 8x \cos 6x - 2\cos 8x \cos 2x &= 0\\
\Rightarrow\qquad -2\cos 8x(\cos 6x + \cos 2x) &= 0\\
\Rightarrow\qquad -2\cos 8x(2\cos 4x \cos 2x) &= 0\\
\Rightarrow\qquad -4\cos 8x \cos 4x \cos 2x &= 0\\
\Rightarrow\qquad \cos 2x \cos 4x \cos 8x &= 0.
\end{align*}Therefore, $a + b + c = \boxed{2 + 4 + 8 = 14}.$
